summary: Gruber investigates reports that different iPhone OS builds (5A345 and 5A347) were causing varying display color temperatures on the iPhone 3G. While Macworld reported Apple confirmed warmer displays were a deliberate design change, Ars Technica claimed the newer build reduced the yellow tinge. Gruber digs into Apple's update XML to show that Apple treats the two builds as identical, and confirms with an Apple engineering source that no display color temperature changes exist between the builds. He concludes the variation is likely due to different screen hardware components, not software.
key_points: Apple's Bob Borchers confirmed the warmer iPhone 3G display color temperature was a deliberate design change
iTunes treats builds 5A345 and 5A347 as identical, with the XML update file explicitly marking 5A345 as 'SameAs' 5A347
An Apple iPhone engineering source confirmed no display color temperature changes exist between the two builds
Display color variation between individual iPhone 3G units is likely caused by different screen hardware components, not software differences
money_quotes: It struck me as very unlikely that Apple would make a change as significant as tweaking the display color temperature at the last minute.
But if they were to make a change like that, it seems even more unlikely that they would do so in a build that isn't pushed out as an automatic update for iPhones running 5A345.
5A345 is explicitly marked as being the same as 5A347, at least for the purposes of recommended software updates.

I asked a source at Apple on the iPhone engineering team who is, as they say, familiar with the situation, and my source told me there were no changes regarding display color temperature between 5A345 and 5A347.
Whatever is causing this variation — my guess is slightly different screen components — isn't related to versions 5A345 and 5A347 of the OS.
key_insight: Display color temperature differences between iPhone 3G units are caused by hardware component variation, not software build differences, despite widespread speculation to the contrary.
